originalTitle: Desperate for food drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: The Amazing Gingerbread Tree: A Helper for Hungry Friends in Africa!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly reporter, Sunny, here to share a very important story from our big, beautiful continent of Africa. Today, we're talking about a country called Kenya, which is far away but still part of our amazing African family. Kenya is a place with lots of sunshine, incredible animals like giraffes and elephants, and lovely people. But right now, some of our friends in Kenya are having a very tough time. They are very, very hungry, and they don't have enough food to eat. This is happening because something called a 'drought' has come to their land.

No Rain, No Food: What is a Drought?

Imagine your favourite plant in your garden or a pot. What does it need to grow big and strong? That's right, water and sunshine! Now, imagine if the rain stopped coming for a very, very long time. Not just for a day or a week, but for many months, or even a whole year! That's what a drought is. It's when there is no rain, and the ground gets super dry and cracked. When there's no rain, plants can't grow. Farmers can't grow yummy foods like maize (which is like corn) or beans. Animals also get very thirsty, and sometimes they don't have enough to eat either. Sadly, many families in Kenya, and in nearby countries like Ethiopia and Somalia, are finding it hard to get food. Millions of people, that's like all the kids in hundreds and hundreds of schools put together, are feeling very hungry because of this long, dry time. Did you know? Water is super important for all living things! You can live without food for a few weeks, but you can't live without water for more than a few days!

Meet the Amazing Gingerbread Tree!

But guess what? Even in tough times, nature can be amazing and help us! In Kenya, there is a very special tree that is helping people find food. It's called the Gingerbread Tree! Isn't that a fun name? It's not a tree that grows actual gingerbread cookies, but its fruit smells a bit sweet and looks a little like a gingerbread man when it's ripe. That's probably how it got its yummy name! This tree is very clever. It can grow in places where it's very dry and hot, where other trees can't survive. It's like a superhero tree that can live in super-tough conditions! Did you know? The Gingerbread Tree is also sometimes called the Doum Palm! It's a type of palm tree, just like where coconuts grow, but this one has different kinds of fruits and roots!

How This Special Tree Helps

When there is no other food, families in Kenya turn to this wonderful Gingerbread Tree. They carefully collect its fruits, which can be eaten, but the most important part they use for food are its roots. Yes, the roots! Grown-ups carefully dig up some of the roots from the ground. These roots are like special storage cupboards for the tree, full of energy and food that the tree has saved up. Once they have the roots, they clean them very well. Then, they cook them. After cooking, these roots can be mashed up or eaten, and they give people some of the energy and strength they need to keep going. It's not a feast, but it helps them not feel so hungry. This tree is truly a lifesaver because it gives food when other food sources have dried up. It shows us how strong and clever nature can be, helping us even when things are difficult.

Are Other Helpers Around?

Yes, absolutely! While the gingerbread tree is a wonderful helper, it's not enough for everyone. Many kind groups, like 'Oxfam' (they are a big helper organisation), are working hard to bring food, clean water, and medicine to the people in Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia. They want to make sure everyone gets enough to eat and drink and stays healthy. These helpers are like a big, caring family from all over the world, reaching out to give a helping hand. They know it's super important for all children and families to have enough food, so they are doing their best to bring relief to those who are suffering from the drought. It's a big job, but they are trying very hard.

What Can We Learn and Do?

This story from Kenya teaches us a few important things. First, it reminds us how precious rain and water are. We should always try to save water! Remember to turn off the tap when you brush your teeth and don't let water run for too long. Second, it shows us how amazing nature is, like the Gingerbread Tree, which can help people in tough times. We should always respect and take care of our planet and its trees. Third, it teaches us to be kind and think about others. Even if we can't go to Kenya ourselves, we can talk to our parents or teachers about groups that help people. We can learn more about different countries and share what we've learned with our friends. Let's all remember our friends in Kenya and other parts of Africa. They are strong, and they are getting help. And the amazing Gingerbread Tree is doing its part too! Thank you for listening, super friends! See you next time!
summary: In parts of Africa where a long drought has made people very hungry, a special tree called the gingerbread tree is helping families find food from its roots and fruit.
whyMatters: It matters because everyone deserves to have enough food and water to be healthy and happy, and this story shows how brave people are, how nature can help, and why it's important for everyone to care for each other.

originalTitle: Chad warns Sudan it will retaliate after drone strike on mourners kills 17
title: Big News from Our Neighbors: Chad and Sudan
content:
Hello, super kids of Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, ready to tell you about something important happening in our big, beautiful continent. Today, we're talking about two countries that are neighbors. These countries are called Chad and Sudan. They are like houses next to each other in a very big village! Sometimes, even good neighbors can have a little trouble. And right now, there's some important news about Chad and Sudan that we need to understand together, in a simple way.

A Sad Day in Chad

Imagine your family and friends gathering together. Maybe it's for a birthday party, or a special celebration. Well, in a place in Chad called Ennedi-Est, people were gathered together too. But they were gathered for a sad reason. They were saying goodbye to someone they loved who had passed away. This is called a funeral, and it's a very sad time. While these people were quietly mourning their friend, something unexpected and scary happened. A special flying robot plane came. This kind of plane is sometimes called a 'drone'. It caused a big problem where the people were gathered. It was a very loud and scary moment for everyone there. Sadly, some people got hurt, and a few people even lost their lives. This made everyone in Chad very, very sad and worried. No one wants to see their friends or family hurt. Did you know? A 'drone' is like a tiny plane that flies without a pilot inside! Someone controls it from far away, using a special remote control. They can be used for many things, like taking cool pictures from the sky or delivering packages, but sometimes, sadly, they can be used to cause trouble.

Chad's Leader is Upset

When the important news reached Chad's leader, President Mahamat Déby, he was very, very upset. He felt sad for the people who were hurt and for their families. He also felt very angry because he thinks the flying robot plane came from Sudan, their neighbor. President Déby spoke out strongly. He said that Chad would 'retaliate'. This is a big word, but it just means that Chad will respond. It means they will do something back to make sure this kind of sad event doesn't happen again. He wants to protect his people. He wants to make sure that everyone in Chad feels safe in their homes and when they are together. A president's job is to look after everyone in their country and keep them safe. Did you know? A President is like the head teacher of a very big school – their country! They make important decisions and try their best to keep everyone safe and happy, just like your head teacher looks after all the students.

Soldiers Are Getting Ready

After this sad event, President Déby gave some very important instructions to Chad's army. The army is a group of brave men and women who protect the country. He told them to be 'on high alert'. Being 'on high alert' means that all the soldiers need to be extra careful. They need to watch everything very closely and be ready for anything that might happen. It's like when you're playing hide-and-seek, and you're extra quiet and looking everywhere for your friends! The soldiers are now working extra hard to make sure that no more flying robot planes cause trouble in Chad. Their main job is to keep all the children, families, and communities safe and sound. They want to prevent any more sad incidents from happening. Did you know? Armies are made up of very strong and disciplined people. They train a lot to be ready to protect their country from any dangers. They are important for keeping peace and order.

The Border is Closed!

Chad and Sudan share a very long 'border'. Imagine drawing a very long, invisible line on the ground between two big backyards. That's what a border is between countries! Because of the trouble, President Déby made another very big decision. He said that the border between Chad and Sudan would be totally closed. This means no one can go from Chad into Sudan, and no one can come from Sudan into Chad for now. It's like when your parents close the gate to your garden. They do it to keep everyone safe inside, and to stop anything unwanted from coming in. Closing the border helps Chad keep its people safe and also stops any more problems from crossing over. This also means that things like trucks carrying food or other goods cannot cross either. It's a big step, but leaders sometimes have to make tough choices to protect their people. Did you know? Africa has many, many countries, and each one has its own borders! These borders help us know where one country ends and another begins, just like fences separate houses.

Why Are Things Tricky Between Neighbors?

Some of you might be wondering, why are Chad and Sudan having trouble? Well, sadly, in Sudan, there has been some fighting happening inside their own country for a while. It's a very difficult situation for the people of Sudan. Sometimes, when there is fighting in one country, it can spill over a little bit into the neighboring country. This is why Chad is extra careful right now. They want to make sure the problems in Sudan don't cause more sadness or harm to the people of Chad. Chad has many friends from Sudan who have come to Chad because they needed a safe place. Chad wants to make sure it stays a safe and welcoming place for everyone, especially for those seeking help. Did you know? It's always important to be kind to our neighbors, both people and countries! When we are kind and help each other, everyone feels safer and happier. We all want peace in our communities.

What Will Happen Next?

This is a very important time. Everyone hopes that the leaders of Chad and Sudan will talk to each other very soon. When people talk, they can understand each other better and try to find solutions to their problems without more fighting. We all wish for peace and friendship to return between these two important neighbors in Africa. It's so much better when countries are friends and work together, just like when you play nicely with your classmates. Your friendly news reporter hopes that very soon, we will hear good news about peace and safety for all the wonderful children and families in Chad and Sudan. Let's send them our thoughts of peace! Did you know? When leaders from different countries talk to each other, it's called 'diplomacy'. It's like having a very serious chat to solve problems and make sure everyone can live peacefully. That's all for today's important news update, super kids! Stay curious and kind!
summary: Chad's President has put the army on high alert and closed the border with Sudan after a drone strike on mourners killed 17 people, warning Sudan that Chad will respond to the attack.
whyMatters: This news is important because when countries that are neighbors have trouble, it can make many people feel worried and unsafe, and it shows why it's so important for leaders to talk and keep peace for everyone.

originalTitle: Denounce 'abject' Afcon decision - senior Caf member
title: Sad News for Senegal: No Big Football Party in 2025!
content:
A Big Football Party!

Imagine a super big, super fun football party! It’s called the Africa Cup of Nations, or AFCON for short. It’s a very important football game where all the best teams from different countries in Africa play against each other. Everyone loves watching their favourite teams score goals and win! This big party happens every two years. It’s a time for joy, excitement, and showing off amazing football skills. People from all over Africa, and even the world, watch these exciting matches. It’s a big celebration of sport and friendship. Countries work very hard to be ready for AFCON. They want to make sure their stadiums are perfect and that everyone has a great time. It’s a huge honour to be the country that gets to host this amazing event. Did you know? Football is the most popular sport in the whole wide world! Lots of children, just like you, love to kick a ball around with their friends.

Senegal's Turn to Host!

Once upon a time, it was Senegal's turn! The wonderful country of Senegal was chosen to host the AFCON party in 2025. This was super exciting news for all the people in Senegal. They were so happy and proud! Being the host country means a lot of special things. It means you get to build big, shiny new stadiums. It means many people from other countries will come and visit your country. They will see your beautiful cities and meet your friendly people. It’s like being chosen to host a big birthday party for all your friends. You get to decide where to put the cake, what games to play, and make sure everyone has a fantastic time. Senegal was ready to do all of that for AFCON 2025. People in Senegal started dreaming about the big games, the cheering crowds, and their country being in the spotlight. Everyone was looking forward to this special time. It was a big dream coming true for Senegal. Did you know? Senegal is a country in West Africa. Its capital city is Dakar, which is a very busy and colourful place near the ocean!

Uh Oh, Bad News!

But then, something unexpected happened. The grown-ups who are in charge of all the African football, called CAF (that stands for the Confederation of African Football), made a new choice. They said that Senegal would not be able to host the AFCON party in 2025 anymore. This news made many people in Senegal very, very sad. It’s like when you’re really excited for a trip to the park, and then at the last minute, your grown-up says you can’t go. It can make you feel disappointed and a little bit confused. The decision was made to take away Senegal's chance to host. So, the big football party for 2025 will now need to find a new home. This was a big change for Senegal and for the plans of AFCON. It can be tough when big plans change, especially when so many people were looking forward to something. Everyone in Senegal had imagined this big event happening right in their own country.

Why is This Sad?

It's sad because a lot of hard work and dreams were put into preparing for AFCON 2025 in Senegal. When you’re chosen to host, you start making many plans. You think about where everyone will stay and how the games will be played. For a country, hosting a big event like AFCON can bring lots of good things. It can make new jobs for people, help build new things like roads and hotels, and make the country feel very important on the world stage. So, when the chance to host is taken away, it feels unfair. It feels like all that excitement and effort might have been for nothing. People can feel upset and wonder why the change happened. It’s natural to feel sad or angry when something you were hoping for, and working towards, suddenly changes. Especially when it’s a big event that so many people were looking forward to celebrating together. Did you know? Senegal’s national football team is called the ‘Lions of Teranga’. They are very strong and even won the AFCON tournament in 2021! They are champions!

Who Made the Decision?

The decision came from CAF. Remember, CAF is like the main boss for all the football games in Africa. They have a special group of important people who make big decisions about football on our continent. This group is called the executive committee. These important people at CAF get together and talk about many things. They decide where the tournaments will be held, what rules to follow, and how to make African football better. They have a very big and important job. So, it was this group of important people who decided that Senegal would not host AFCON 2025. It was their choice, and it changed the plans for the big football party. Sometimes grown-ups have to make tough choices, even if they make some people sad. But for the people of Senegal, this choice was very difficult to understand and accept.

Mr. Senghor is Upset!

One of the important people in CAF is named Augustin Senghor. He is from Senegal, and he is a very important part of the CAF executive committee. So, when this decision was made, he was very upset. Mr. Senghor said that the decision to take away Senegal's hosting rights was "unacceptable" and "abject." These are strong words! When he says "unacceptable," it means it's not okay and he doesn't agree with it at all. When he says "abject," it means he thinks it's a very, very bad and unfair decision. He feels like it was a really wrong choice to make. He believes Senegal deserved to host the party. He is speaking out because he cares deeply about his country and about fair play in football. He wants everyone to know that he thinks this decision was not right. He is showing how strongly he feels.

What Does 'Denounce' Mean?

Mr. Senghor also said, "we have to denounce it." What does it mean to "denounce" something? It means to speak out very loudly and clearly that you think something is wrong. It means to tell everyone that you disagree and that you think it's not fair. When you denounce something, you are saying, "Hey! This is not right! We don't agree with this!" It’s a way to show that you are unhappy and want things to change, or at least want people to know your feelings. So, Mr. Senghor wants to make sure that everyone knows he believes this decision was wrong. He wants to make a strong point so that maybe, in the future, things can be handled more fairly. He is using his voice to stand up for Senegal. Did you know? The AFCON trophy is a beautiful golden cup! The winning team gets to lift it high and celebrate their amazing victory!

Keep Playing, Senegal!

Even though Senegal won't host the big party in 2025, it doesn't mean they will stop being a great football country! Senegal has an amazing football team, and they work very hard. They have many talented players who love the game. They have won the AFCON before, which shows how strong and talented they are. They will keep practicing, keep playing, and keep dreaming of winning more trophies. This setback will not stop them from being champions on the field. Sometimes, plans change, and that can be hard. But brave people and strong teams learn to keep going. They remember their dreams and work even harder for the next time. Senegal will surely continue to be a fantastic football nation. Their fans will keep cheering for them, and the Lions of Teranga will keep making their country proud with their skills and teamwork. They will keep their spirits high.

What Happens Next?

So, what happens now for AFCON 2025? Well, CAF will need to find a new country to host the big football party. They will look at other countries in Africa to see which one is ready and able to put on a great tournament. It’s like if your first choice for a party venue doesn’t work out, you have to look for another one! CAF will start this process again to make sure that the AFCON 2025 can still happen and be a wonderful event for everyone. Soon, we will hear news about which country will be the new host. The most important thing is that the spirit of African football continues, and we get to enjoy many exciting games. Did you know? The first AFCON tournament was held in 1957! That’s a very, very long time ago!

Football Fun Continues!

It's a little sad that Senegal won't host in 2025, but football is still fun! We learn that sometimes things change, and it’s okay to feel sad or upset. But it’s also important to remember that the game goes on. We will still watch incredible African football. We will still cheer for our favourite teams. And we will still celebrate the joy and excitement that this beautiful sport brings to all of us. Let's keep our spirits high and look forward to more amazing football memories!
summary: Senegal will no longer host the big Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) football party in 2025, which made an important football leader, Augustin Senghor, very upset as he feels it is an unfair decision.
whyMatters: This matters because hosting the AFCON is a huge honour and a big dream for any country, bringing excitement, visitors, and new buildings, so taking it away from Senegal is a sad and unfair change for many people.

title: Who will be England's World Cup number 10? Tuchel's selection dilemmas
content: The England squad Thomas Tuchel names on Friday will give a big hint about who will be on the plane to the World Cup - so what are the issues he still needs to resolve?
